terms.definition: A wheelchair - a special chair with wheels that helps people who cannot walk to move around. The word 'pahiya' means wheel and 'kursi' means chair. Sunita uses a pahiya kursi because she cannot walk on her own.
terms.exampleUsage: सुनीता अपनी पहिया कुर्सी से स्कूल जाती है। (Sunita goes to school in her wheelchair.)

terms.definition: Disability - a condition where a part of the body does not work as expected. It can affect walking, seeing, hearing, or other abilities. Having a disability does not mean a person cannot do great things, as Sunita's story shows.
terms.exampleUsage: विकलांगता कोई कमज़ोरी नहीं है। (Disability is not a weakness.)

terms.definition: Courage or bravery - the quality of being able to face difficult or scary situations without giving up. Sunita shows great himmat by living her life fully despite not being able to walk.
terms.exampleUsage: सुनीता ने बहुत हिम्मत दिखाई। (Sunita showed great courage.)

terms.definition: Self-confidence - believing in yourself and your abilities. 'Aatm' means self and 'vishvas' means trust or belief. Sunita has strong aatmvishvas which helps her overcome challenges every day.
terms.exampleUsage: आत्मविश्वास से हर मुश्किल आसान हो जाती है। (With self-confidence, every difficulty becomes easy.)

terms.definition: Empathy or sympathy - the ability to understand and share the feelings of another person. It is different from pity. The story teaches us to show sahanubhuti, not tars (pity), towards people with disabilities.
terms.exampleUsage: हमें दूसरों के प्रति सहानुभूति रखनी चाहिए। (We should have empathy towards others.)

terms.definition: A respectful Hindi word for a differently-abled person. 'Divy' means divine and 'ang' means body part. This word is used instead of 'viklang' (disabled) to show respect. It means a person with divine or special body parts.
terms.exampleUsage: दिव्यांग व्यक्ति भी बड़े-बड़े काम कर सकते हैं। (Differently-abled people can also do great things.)

terms.definition: Struggle or fight - the effort needed to overcome difficulties. Sunita's life is a sangharsh against her physical limitations, but she faces it bravely every day. Sangharsh makes a person stronger.
terms.exampleUsage: सुनीता का संघर्ष हम सबके लिए प्रेरणा है। (Sunita's struggle is an inspiration for all of us.)

terms.definition: Inspiration - something or someone that gives you the idea or motivation to do something good. Sunita is a prerana (inspiration) for all children because she never gives up despite facing challenges.
terms.exampleUsage: सुनीता की कहानी बहुत प्रेरणादायक है। (Sunita's story is very inspiring.)

terms.definition: Equality - treating everyone the same way regardless of their physical abilities, appearance, or background. The story teaches us that all people deserve samanta and respect, whether or not they have a disability.
terms.exampleUsage: हर इंसान को समानता का अधिकार है। (Every person has the right to equality.)

terms.definition: Respect or honour - treating someone with dignity and valuing them. We should give samman to every person, including those with disabilities. Feeling sorry for someone is not the same as respecting them.
terms.exampleUsage: हमें सबका सम्मान करना चाहिए। (We should respect everyone.)
